title: WIP: [CI:DOCS] Cirrus: Implement new retrospective task
body:
***NOTE***: Sadly there's no way to actually test this before merging it.  However, there is extensive testing enabled [at the source](https://github.com/containers/automation), including self-testing changes to the cirrus-ci_retrospective container image itself.

For reasons such as releasing software or notifying people
about various conditions, a method is required to detect
and execute commands/scripts when all Cirrus-CI operations
have definitively concluded.  However, due to the nature of
automation, there are occasional and unpredictable needs
to manually perform these activities.

Given existing familiarity and dependence on Cirrus-CI, add a
'retrospective' task with a 'manual' trigger and no dependencies.
Remove other notification/monitoring operations in anticipation
this task will take them over in a future commit.  Add a
github action that will atomicly and trigger the new task
on branch and tag push, under one of two conditions:

* The new github Action detects the Cirrus-CI 'success' task
  finished running and was not manually canceled.
* A person pressed the manual 'trigger' button on the new
  'retrospective' task, from the status page, on any build.

Also: E-mail me immediatly if there is ever a problem with the
      magic post-cirrus triggering automation.

Signed-off-by: Chris Evich <cevich@redhat.com>

comments.body:
@edsantiago @rhatdan @mheon PTAL.  Once working, my intention is to use the new `retrospective` Cirrus-CI task for our branch failure notifications and for building/uploading releases.  

Unfortunately, due to security limitations (that we actually want) the github action-magic cannot be tested in a PR, it __only__ runs directly from the master branch.  If it helps, I can clone this PR over into [automation_sandbox](https://github.com/containers/automation_sandbox) and merge it there first.  Let me know if that would be helpful.

@edsantiago @rhatdan @mheon PTAL at a clone of this repo. where both Cirrus-CI and the new github action are operating as intended on the master branch (after push):

* Cirrus-CI run: https://github.com/containers/automation_sandbox/runs/577159168
* Github Action: https://github.com/containers/automation_sandbox/actions/runs/75323865

The action detected conclusion of the Cirrus-CI `success` task, and subsequently triggered the manual `retrospective` task (where releases, notifications, or other follow-up activities will happen)

Here's an example pull-request https://github.com/containers/automation_sandbox/pull/81/checks and the corresponding github action: https://github.com/containers/automation_sandbox/runs/577261181?check_suite_focus=true

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *cevich*, *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably #5855) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.

***Update:*** This PR is blocked on debugging/investigating a status race or sync. problem regarding tag-push.  Since this code can only run from the master branch (by design) I need to do that work in a sandbox repository.
@cevich Anything goin on with this PR?

> @cevich Anything goin on with this PR?

Yes.  I might close it :cry: or re-implement it to deal with the new reality: We cannot rely on tag-push triggering of Cirrus-CI workflow that is accountable on the Github side (like for PR's and Branches).

We've got an internal e-mail thread going with @mheon and @baude (and you) about needs relating to release automation.  Choices made there will impact the re-implementation or closure of this PR.

This topic is a top-priority of mine at the moment.
A friendly reminder that this PR had no activity for 30 days.
I'm going to close this for now in favor of a new PR (coming soon) that will do something less fragile for tags.
